creator: mjg@FreeBSD.org
text:
The entire pipe_kva business probably wants to be eliminated.

I did not have time to do it myself.

I collected some sizes, for most real-world pipes total writes are *very small* (literally bytes) and have 0 reason to use any big-buffer storage.

Not only that, the code to map user pages on I/O is mostly *SLOWER* than merely doing a plain copy into the interim buffer. Some massaging needs to be done, but all this magic (and associated complexity) needs to be eliminated.

In the meantime, a quick hack would indeed alloc a small buf (literally even 512 bytes would do) and use that if pipe_kva is exhausted.
